gate_verdict.reason: admission net=+5, coverage net=+1; admission gained AND coverage did not regress

iterate1_patch_rationale.background: Initial Gate B apply (apply_20260424_bench) introduced 2 admission regressions: Sandmeyer 1237 and Schmidt 1240. Inspection revealed both SMILES lack the reagent atoms the new guards hard-required (anilineA→ArCl has no diazonium atom; benzophenone→amide has no azide atom). Hard penalties therefore fired on legitimate cases.
iterate1_patch_rationale.fix: Converted both guards from hard-reagent-required to positive-boost-only pattern: keep the product-signature penalty (aryl_halide absent / carboxylic_acid+carbonyl absent) but drop the reagent-missing penalty, and boost when the precursor feature IS present. This matches the Category A 'reagent channel' principle — don't demote for missing reagents, only reward when reagents are present.

watchlist_top1_shifts_no_flag_change.description: Cases where top1 prediction changed but pass/fail did not flip. None harmful in isolation; flagged for possible future tuning.

context.resolves_task_39: Task #39 (Cope/Retro-Claisen regressions from 3e-b-1) now partially addressed: Cope Elimination gained 3 cases (1 admission + 2 coverage) thanks to G01_cope_elimination. Retro-Claisen unchanged.
context.eval_harness: scripts/gateB_eval_harness.py (task #121) used end-to-end: selftest PASS, diff produced edges_summary.json + regression_trace_matrix.csv + per-layer case_diff.csv
